questions.question: In a certain code BLACK is coded as YCZLP and SHINE is coded as HNRHV. What will be the code for WHITE?
questions.explanation: The 1st, 3rd and 5th letters are replaced by their complimentary alphabets. Reverse alphabet of A is Z, B is Y and so on. The 2nd and 4th alphabets interchange positions. WHITE: DTRHV

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The sum of the digits is 11 2+4+5=11 8+0+3=11 1+3+7=11 1+9+3=13 and not 11

questions.question: Arrange the words given below in a meaningful sequence. 1. Polling 2. Counting 3. Result 4. Nomination 5. Campaign
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: 45123. Nomination, campaign, polling, counting, result.

questions.question: Which of the following will be the mirror image of the given question figure, if the mirror is placed along the line MN. Choose the correct mirror image among (I), (II), (III) and (IV) given below the question figure.
From the given figures (I), (II), (III) and (IV), select the one in which question figure (X) is hidden/embedded.
Select the set of numbers that is similar to the following set of numbers. (83, 55, 36)
questions.explanation: The logic followed in the given set is - Sum of digits of first number - 1 = sum of digits of second number And, sum of digits of second number - 1 = sum of digits of third number 8+3=11 5+5=10 3+6=9 Similarly, 1+0+9=10 2+7=9 7+1=8

questions.question: Identify the diagram that best represents the relationship among the given classes. Algebra, Trigonometry, Mathematics
questions.explanation: Algebra and trigonometry are all mathematics, but, these both are independent of each other hence, I.

questions.question: Select the figure that will come next in the following series.
questions.explanation: Firstly, the figure is reflected along y-axis and then, along x-axis and it goes on. And, in each alternate step, circles become 1 and 2 in number.

questions.question: In the following question, select the related letters from the given alternatives. SKNR : OP ∷ ACHJ : ?
questions.explanation: The letter that comes exactly in between the series from S and K is O and that between N and R is P. Similarly, between A and C is B and, between H and J is I. Become, BI.

questions.question: How many squares/rectangles are there in the given figure?
questions.explanation: Squares/rectangles are - EATR, EBUR, EGXW, EACW, EBDW, FAJI, FBKI, ABKJ, ABUT, ABDC, AGXC, BGXD, HJTS, HKUS, HLVS, JKUT, JLVT, JKDC, KLVU, RTCW, RUDW, TUDC. Hence, 22 squares/rectangles.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd letters from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Third letter = first letter - 1 And, fourth letter = second letter + 1 O - 1= N; R + 1= S J - 1 = I; L + 1= M H - 1 = G; S + 1= T Option (d) doesn't follow the same logic, hence, odd.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Resistance is a term in physics, and not a unit. Rest others are Units used to measure different entities in physics.

questions.question: In the following question, from the given alternative words, select the word which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word. DISCLAMATION
questions.explanation: IMITATION cannot be formed.

questions.question: In the following question, correct the equation by interchanging two numbers on same side. 10 X 3 - 20 + 60 ÷ 12 = 72 - 2 X 6 + 18 ÷ 9 + 1
questions.explanation: On interchanging 72 and 18 on RHS, we get, RHS = 18 - 2 X 6 + 72 ÷ 9 + 1 = 18 - 12 + 8 + 1 = 6 + 8 + 1 = 15 = LHS LHS = 30 - 20 + 5 = 30 - 15 = 15

questions.question: Which of the following figures among I, II, III and IV depict the correct cube obtained on folding the given unfolded hollow cube (X)?
questions.explanation: III is the cube obtained on folding the given unfolded cube. The cube is folded as follows:

questions.question: If A-B means A is the brother of B, A*B means A is the wife of B, A+B means A is the sister of B, A÷B means A is the father of B. Then in which of the following given options, C is the daughter of A?
questions.explanation: A is the wife of B and B is the father of C. C is the sister of D which means C is the daughter of A & B. option a is correct

questions.question: Select the word pair in which the two words are related in the same way as are the two words in the following word pair. CANVAS : PAINTER
questions.explanation: The main medium of work of a painter is canvas. Similarly that of a sculptor is stone.

questions.question: Which set of letters when sequentially placed at the gaps in the given letter series shall complete it? a b _ _ a _ c _ a _
questions.explanation: The correct sequence is abcdabcdab.

questions.question: In a certain code if 'M' denotes'÷', 'N' denotes 'X', 'O' denotes '+' and 'P' denotes '-', then what will be the value of 36M9N3O2P7M7N12?
questions.explanation: 36M9N3O2P7M7N12= 36÷9x3+2-7÷7x12 Using BODMAS rule, we get = 4x3+2-12 = 12+2-12 = 14-12 = 2

questions.question: The average age of 29 friends in a batch is 21 years. A new friend joins the batch, if the age of the a new friend is included then the average increases by 1. What is the age of the newly added friend?
questions.explanation: Age of the newly added friend = 30 * 22 - 29 * 21 = 660 - 609 = 51 years

questions.question: In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: All looks are pills. All roots are looks. All oceans are roots. Conclusions: I. All looks are oceans. II. All oceans are pills.
options: If either conclusion I or conclusion II follows.
If neither conclusion I nor conclusion II follows.
questions.explanation: Conclusion II follows.

questions.question: Find the next term in the series. 30, 68, 130, 222, 350, ?
If in a certain code "RANGE" is coded as 12345 and "RANDOM" is coded as 123678, then the code for the word "MANGO" would be
questions.explanation: From the question, R A N G E ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ 1 2 3 4 5 R A N D O M ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ 1 2 3 6 7 8 So we can find that the code letters as, M A N G O ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ 8 2 3 4 7 Hence the correct is option d.

questions.question: In the following question, four number pairs are given. The number on the left side of (:) is related to the number of the right side of (:) with some logic/rule/relation. Three are similar on the basis of the same logic/rule/relation. Select the odd one out from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The 2nd number is reverse of the first number. So, option c is odd.

questions.question: A group of 5 weight-lifters A, B, C, D and E took part in a competition. Each person lifted a different weight. Only one person lifted a weight more than that lifted by E. Two persons lifted weight less than that lifted by C but more than that lifted by B. D did not lift the least amount of weight. How many persons lifted weight less than that lifted by A?
questions.explanation: • Only one person lifted a weight more than that lifted by E. • Two persons lifted weight less than that lifted by C but more than that lifted by B. • D did not lift the least amount of weight.
